title: heterolepis peduncularis
intro: Heterolepis peduncularis, also known as the hairy mountain clover, is a perennial herb native to the high-altitude grasslands of the Andes. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Heterolepis peduncularis include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing and immune system function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, with the leaves and flowers being the primary components used for medicinal purposes.
